title: This 1970s box solves this equation faster than your laptop
description:
This 1970s analog computer — a Comdyna GP-6 — solves a differential equation faster than your laptop. No chip. No code. No software. You wire it up with patch cords and turn knobs, and the machine *becomes* the equation.

Here it's "flying a ball" — solving projectile motion (d²y/dt² = −g) in real time on an oscilloscope. Turn the gravity knob down and the arc stretches out (that's the Moon). Crank it up and it slams down. Nothing re-runs — the wiring IS the program. This is how engineers flew Apollo and guided missiles for 30 years, before digital won on precision.

You can see this exact machine running at the Vintage Computer Federation (VCF) Museum @ InfoAge Science & History Center — on the former Army Signal Corps base in Wall, New Jersey, where they helped invent the radar that won World War II. Run by volunteers. Open weekends. You've probably driven right past the sign.

📍 VCF Museum @ InfoAge — Wall, NJ
🔗 https://infoage.org
